# Alexandria
# Alexandria

> AI-native diligence engine for biopharma M&A and licensing deals. Turns weeks of manual diligence into ten-minute decision-ready deliverables.

Alexandria cross-references a continuously refreshed corpus of pharma research, clinical trials, patents, regulatory filings, and deal comps — alongside a user's own data room — then runs the analysis (rNPV, Monte Carlo, key metrics and indicators) and returns the output in the user's existing deliverable format. The interface is dynamic: the workspace adapts to the analyst's intent rather than returning generic 200-row tables.

Primary users: investment banking healthcare teams, life-sciences consulting practices, and internal biopharma BD&L teams running licensing and M&A diligence.

## Pages

- [Home](/): Product overview, dynamic workspace demo, rNPV and Monte Carlo capabilities, secure data-room workflow, FAQ, and contact.
- [Insights](/insights): Long-form notes on biopharma licensing and M&A diligence, valuation methods, and deal strategy.

## Insights

- [rNPV biotech valuation guide](/insights/rnpv-biotech-valuation): How to model risk-adjusted NPV for a biotech asset, with phase transition probabilities and a worked Phase 2 oncology example.
- [Monte Carlo for licensing deals](/insights/monte-carlo-licensing-deals): Why a single rNPV loses negotiation leverage and how Monte Carlo simulation produces the distribution BD and IB teams negotiate against.
- [Biopharma M&A diligence checklist](/insights/biopharma-diligence-checklist): The working diligence checklist used in practice — science, IP, regulatory, CMC, commercial, and financial.
- [Licensing vs. acquisition](/insights/licensing-vs-acquisition): The framework BD teams use to decide between in-licensing a single asset and acquiring the company that owns it.

## Optional

- [Custom domain](https://www.www.alexandrialabs.uk): Primary marketing domain.
